Defendant Santa Fe Braun, Inc.¿s joinder to defendant Republic Supply Co.¿s motion to dismiss for delay in prosecution is granted. No opposition filed. rulings.Case Title: CECIL STEPHENS VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Motion Calendar for Wednesday, June 8, 2011, line 2. Defendant Crosby Valve, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is off calendar at the moving party¿s request. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: CECIL STEPHENS VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Motion Calendar for Wednesday, June 8, 2011, line 2. DEFENDANT FLOWSERVE CORPORATION SUCCESSOR-IN-INTEREST to Durametallic Corp.¿s motion for summary judgment and alternative motion for summary adjudication will be heard in Department 503 at 1:30pm, Hon. Teri L. Jackson presiding. Defendant¿s motion for summary judgment and alternative motion for summary adjudication are denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Cecil Stephens was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiff¿s deposition testimony, where he identified Mr. Stephens¿ co-workers who had information about Mr. Stephens¿ work with Durametallic products, precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar, as defendant¿s separate statement did not demonstrate that co-workers did not have such knowledge. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production as to each and every adjudication issue. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 1:30pm. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: CECIL STEPHENS VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Motion Calendar for Wednesday, June 8, 2011, line 2. Defendant Red-White Valve Corp.¿s motion for summary judgment and alternative motion for summary adjudication will be heard in Department 503 at 1:30pm, Hon. Teri L. Jackson presiding. Defendant¿s motion for summary judgment and alternative motion for summary adjudication are denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Cecil Stephens was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiff¿s responses to defendant¿s special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Plaintiff¿s objections to the declaration of Mr. White are sustained. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production as to all adjudication issues. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 1:30pm. = (503/TLJ)
